steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is an Inferences question asking what an ecosystem-based approach to conserving M. guerreronis “could thus” do. The decisive clues are that the ecosystem approach “incorporates the many interactions between climate, terrain, and various species” and “may lead to better outcomes for all the species” — meaning strategies that are not narrowly targeted at one species can still protect it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘allow conservationists to better consider how climatic changes affecting Guerrero may reduce the number of species competing with M. guerreronis.’: Introduces the idea of reducing competing species, which is not mentioned or implied in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘identify conservation strategies that may not be exclusively aimed at protecting M. guerreronis but that benefit the species nonetheless.’: Directly reflects the passage’s logic: broader ecosystem strategies produce better outcomes for all species, including M. guerreronis, even without targeting it alone. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘lead to a better understanding of how the distribution of Mammillaria species throughout Mexico has affected their survival.’: Shifts scope to the entire genus across Mexico, which goes beyond what the passage claims about ecosystem-based conservation in Guerrero. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘be unable to evaluate whether M. guerreronis would be capable of surviving in habitats outside of Guerrero.’: Concludes the approach would be unable to do something — a negative outcome that contradicts the passage’s claim that the ecosystem approach leads to “better outcomes.” ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
